_about: Mock numbers for the 'Scalability story' page (web-demo/scalability.html). All values are synthetic and editable in one place: changing a number here rewrites every tile and re-shapes the chart on the page automatically. See web-demo/scalability.js for derived-value formulas. Schema v2: on-prem framing — instances no longer carry an hourly $ rate; economics are expressed as 'API cost avoided per day' against a Frontier-API comparator. v2 also adds optional `nodes_per_rack` and `composition[]` fields on instances so a single 'instance' can stand in for a node OR for a fully populated rack; the rack_capacity tile reads those fields.

comparators.label: Frontier LLM API (per-call)
comparators.notes: Synthetic: 1500 input + 800 output tokens at a typical $1 / $3 per 1M token rate (~Haiku-class pricing). Does not include orchestration, retries, or egress.

comparators.label: Frontier LLM API (research-class)
comparators.notes: Synthetic: ~12k input + 3k output tokens per research task at premium model pricing. Excludes vector store and retrieval costs.

instances.label: Intel Xeon CWF
instances.subtitle: 1 node · 576 vCPU · 1536 GB
instances.notes: Dual-socket Clearwater Forest reference: 288 E-cores per socket, ~3 GB / core. Owned hardware — no cloud hourly rate; capital and power costs are out of scope for this page.

instances.label: Intel GNR
instances.subtitle: 1 node · 256 vCPU · 1024 GB
instances.notes: Dual-socket Granite Rapids reference: 128 P-cores per socket, ~4 GB / core. AMX accelerates the SLM offload path. Owned hardware — no cloud hourly rate.

instances.label: Intel Xeon CWF rack
instances.subtitle: 32 nodes · 18,432 vCPU · 49,152 GB
instances.notes: 42U rack populated with 32× 1U dual-socket Clearwater Forest nodes (remaining U is leaf switches and PDUs). Per-node sizing identical to the single-node row. Linear scale-out assumes the front-door router fans agent sessions across nodes; cross-node coordination cost is folded into the queueing curve.

instances.label: Intel GNR rack
instances.subtitle: 32 nodes · 8,192 vCPU · 32,768 GB
instances.notes: 42U rack populated with 32× 1U dual-socket Granite Rapids nodes. AMX accelerates per-node SLM throughput; vLLM is sharded by node, not across nodes. Same per-node curve as the single-node GNR row, fanned across 32 nodes by the LiteLLM/router layer.

instances.label: Mixed rack — agent half (CWF)
instances.subtitle: 16× CWF agent half · 16× GNR offload half
instances.notes: Mixed-SKU rack: CWF half hosts agent session pods (high core count, low per-core memory); GNR half hosts vLLM and the analytics offload workers (AMX-accelerated). The density tile reflects only the CWF agent half — the 16 GNR nodes do not host agents, they absorb the offloaded inference and analytics load. Total rack capacity (CWF + GNR) is shown by the rack-capacity tile.

workloads.label: Terminal agent (small)
workloads.subtitle: engineering / shell tasks
workloads.notes: One agent ≈ 1 vCPU + 2 GB resident; per-task wall-clock 8s end-to-end including a single LLM round-trip. Human equivalent: a junior engineer running the same shell task by hand.

workloads.label: Market research (medium)
workloads.subtitle: multi-step research + offload
workloads.notes: Research agent that does retrieval, multi-call summarization, and writes a short report. 4 vCPU and 45s wall-clock are typical when offload keeps the heavy step off the agent pod.

scenarios.label: Xeon CWF · 1 node · small agents
scenarios.story: How dense a single dual-socket CWF node gets when packed with light terminal agents — and how much frontier-API spend that displaces.

scenarios.label: GNR · 1 node · research workload
scenarios.story: Heavier research agents on a GNR node with multi-call inference and offload — same density story, different price-point.

scenarios.label: Xeon CWF rack · 32 nodes · small agents
scenarios.story: Linear scale-out of the per-node story. A full 42U rack of dual-socket CWF nodes packs ~18k concurrent terminal agents and shifts the daily volume / displaced-API-spend tiles by the same node multiplier.

scenarios.label: GNR rack · 32 nodes · research workload
scenarios.story: Same research workload as the single-node row, fanned across a full GNR rack. Each node serves its own vLLM + offload stack; LiteLLM / the front-door router handles routing.

scenarios.label: Mixed rack · 16× CWF agent + 16× GNR offload
scenarios.story: Half of the rack runs agent session pods on CWF (high core count); the other half runs the SLM and analytics offload on GNR (AMX-accelerated). The density numbers below are for the CWF agent half — see the rack-capacity tile for the full 32-node footprint.
